text.title: CI Quality Gate Design Procedure
text.summary: Five-step procedure from "we have a CI we don't trust" to "ci-design.json committed + gates enforce the design".

step.name: inventory
step.action: List every check with its name, runtime_p50_min, current pass/fail rate, current block/warn semantics.
step.decision-gate: If a check is undocumented (no name in YAML or hidden behind a script) — STOP, document first.

step.name: classify
step.action: Assign tier per rubric: high-signal fast → BLOCK; medium-signal infra/style → WARN; slow / exploratory → NIGHTLY. Owner per check.
step.decision-gate: If a high-signal slow check (e.g. e2e) lands in BLOCK and blows the budget — STOP, parallelize or demote.

step.name: budget
step.action: Compute block_critical_path_min = longest chain through BLOCK with parallel groups collapsed. Compare to target_min.
step.output: budget block with headroom or deficit.
step.decision-gate: If headroom < 0 AND no remediation — STOP, verdict block-budget-exceeded.

step.name: rationale
step.action: Write one paragraph: what this check catches, why it BLOCK and not WARN, what the workaround is.
step.output: rationale field populated per BLOCK check.
step.decision-gate: If any BLOCK check lacks rationale — STOP, verdict block-missing-rationale.

step.name: commit
step.action: Validate against schema. Commit ci-design.json next to CI config. Open PR; gate refuses CI changes without an updated artefact.
step.decision-gate: If CI YAML drifts from artefact (tier mismatch, owner change) — STOP, fix one or the other.
